WHEN the news of the capture of Sedan arrived at Stock-
holm, the King's Theatre was ordered to be closed for that
evening.
SIR TITUS SALT, of Saltaire, was presented on Saturday
with a testimonial, consisting of pieces of plate, subscribed for
by the children of the village connected with his factory.
A RUMOUR is current that Mr. C. Wykeham Martin, M.P.,
is about to retire from the representation of Newport, Isle of
Wight.
Miss EMILY FAITHFULL has been elected a juror in Class 15,
Textile Fabrics, of the Working Men's International Exhi-
bition.

ocr_hypothesis.transcription_unit:
PARTIAL DESTRUCTION OF A COTTON MILL EY FIRE.
—The large spinning and weaving mill of Mr. Shepley,
at Brook Field, near Glossop, Derbyshire, was dis-
covered to be on fire on Wednesday night last, and
burnt with such rapidity that within twenty minutes of
the discovery the roof had fallen in. The neighbouring
mill-owners lent assistance by sending their fire engines
and hands to extinguish the flames, but this was not
effected till about 3,000/. worth of property was de-
stroyed.

ground_truth.transcription_unit:
TO be SOLD, an ESTATE, containing 450
ACRES, or thereabouts (of which about 50 Acres are
in Coppices) of the yearly Value of £. 300, with an unlimited
Right over very good and extensive Commons, and about
£.3000 worth of Timber thereon, in a very growing State,
paying at least £.7 per Cent. The Tenure is Copyhold of
Inheritance, under a small and certain Fine, and is esteemed
equal in Value to Freehold. It lies about seven Miles from
Southampton, from one to two Miles of the London Turnpike
Road, and within about two miles of the flowing of the Sea: it will
receive great addition on an Inclosure of the Commons, and it
is covenanted there will be no Expence for Repairs during four¬
teen Years to come.
Enquire of Messrs. Maberly and Daniell, King's Road, corner
of Bedford-row, London.

ocr_hypothesis.transcription_unit:
The official Bajerische Zeitung says :
"The statements of some German papers that
an understanding bad been arrived at between
Bavaria and Prussia, respecting the proposals of
the latter for a reform of the Federal Constitu-
tion, are entirely devoid of foundation.
"The Bavarian Government is as unacquainted
with the nature of these proposals as are the
Governments of the other German States; but
it is ready to examine them whenever they shall
be submitted to its consideration."

with the nature of these proposals as are the
Governments of the other German States; but
it is ready to examine them whenever they shall
be submitted to its consideration."
